steps.title: Turn the information into two points
steps.content: The function has the form $j(x)=mx+144$, so its y-intercept is $144$. That gives the point $(0,144)$. The statement $j(12)=18$ gives the point $(12,18)$.

steps.title: Use linear regression in Desmos
steps.content: Enter those two points and regress a line. Desmos returns $m=-10.5$ and $b=144$.

steps.title: Evaluate at x = 10
steps.content:
Now compute:
$$j(10)=-10.5(10)+144=-105+144=39$$
In Desmos, $10m+b$ gives the same value after the regression.
